skill.name: data-storytelling
skill.description: Transform analytics, metrics, and research findings into clear narratives for reports, dashboards, executive updates, and decision presentations.
skill.summary: Turn raw data into focused business stories with context, visuals, insights, and action-oriented recommendations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command execution alerts are false positives caused by markdown code fences, presentation templates, diagrams, and a plotting example. The only confirmed risk factor is low-risk network exposure from three external resource links, with no evidence of automatic requests, exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Resource Links
low_findings.description: The skill includes three hardcoded reference URLs in the Resources section. They are user-visible learning links, not automatic network calls, so the risk is limited to normal outbound browsing if a user chooses to open them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are clearly present in the Resources section. There is no evidence that the skill fetches these URLs or sends data to them.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks and Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged multiple markdown blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The reviewed lines are documentation examples, narrative templates, diagrams, and Python plotting guidance, with no executable shell command or instruction to run a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is either an inline markdown reference or a code fence delimiter around instructional content. No shell metacharacter execution, command invocation, or user-input execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Crypto and Reconnaissance Keywords
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are keyword matches in descriptive prose, business metrics, and an external book link. No evidence found of cryptographic API usage, host probing, environment discovery, or system inventory collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines are narrative description, table values, recommendations, or a reference URL. They do not contain code capable of cryptography, scanning, or reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose important insights in dense reports and unfocused charts. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code shape data into clear stories with context, visuals, recommendations, and next steps.
actual_capabilities: Structures data presentations with setup, conflict, resolution, and action steps.
Provides frameworks for churn, performance, market opportunity, and business review stories.
Suggests visualization approaches such as progressive reveal, comparison views, annotations, and highlights.
Offers slide and dashboard story templates for executive summaries and monthly reviews.
Improves headlines, transitions, caveats, and recommendations for non-technical audiences.
Helps connect metrics to business impact, investment requests, and expected outcomes.
limitations: Does not fetch, clean, or validate source data automatically.
Does not create finished slide decks or dashboards by itself.
Requires accurate metrics, audience context, and business goals from the user.
Provides communication guidance, not statistical proof or causal validation.

use_cases.title: Executive Performance Review
use_cases.description: Convert quarterly metrics into a concise narrative with key changes, root causes, risks, and decisions needed.

use_cases.title: Customer Churn Recommendation
use_cases.description: Frame retention data as a problem, insight, solution, expected impact, and budget request.

use_cases.title: Market Expansion Story
use_cases.description: Compare options using weighted factors, show the business tradeoffs, and present a clear recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize One Insight
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short narrative from a small metric set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-storytelling skill to turn these metrics into one clear business insight. Include context, the main finding, and one recommended next step.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Executive Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a leadership-ready update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-storytelling skill to structure an executive update from these metrics. Lead with the so what, explain the trend, identify risks, and end with decisions needed.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Dashboard Narrative
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a one-page dashboard story.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-storytelling skill to design a one-page dashboard narrative. Define the headline, key metric groups, visual emphasis, annotations, caveats, and action section.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Board-Level Recommendation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for persuasive decision support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-storytelling skill to turn this analysis into a board-level recommendation. Compare options, explain tradeoffs, show expected impact, state confidence, and propose the approval ask.

output_examples.input: Monthly revenue rose 15 percent, but customer acquisition cost rose 22 percent and lifetime value rose 8 percent.
output_examples.output: Headline: Revenue growth is healthy, but acquisition efficiency is weakening. The story should compare growth quality, highlight the widening CAC and LTV gap, and recommend channel rebalancing before margin pressure grows.

output_examples.input: Churn is highest among customers with low onboarding engagement during the first 14 days.
output: Hook: Preventable early churn is reducing customer lifetime value.
Evidence: Low first-14-day engagement is linked to higher churn.
Recommendation: Add proactive onboarding outreach and adoption tracking.
Ask: Approve a focused onboarding improvement plan.

output_examples.input: A team needs to present EMEA and APAC expansion options to leadership.
output_examples.output: A strong output would use a comparison story: state the decision, score the markets, explain the biggest tradeoffs, recommend one region, and name the risks to mitigate.

best_practices: Start with the business question and audience decision before choosing visuals.
Lead with the main insight, then use data to support and qualify it.
End every story with a specific action, owner, or decision request.
anti_patterns: Do not paste every metric without selecting the few that support the decision.
Do not open with methodology when the audience needs business impact first.
Do not overstate causation when the data only supports correlation.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create data narratives for reports, dashboards, business reviews, investor updates, and executive presentations.

faq.question: Can it analyze my raw dataset?
faq.answer: It can help interpret provided metrics, but it does not automatically clean or validate raw data.

faq.question: Does it generate charts?
faq.answer: It suggests chart types, annotations, and visual structure. Finished chart creation depends on the tool and data you provide.

faq.question: Who is the best audience for this skill?
faq.answer: It is best for analysts, product managers, strategy teams, and leaders who explain data to decision makers.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for shaping analysis into clear data stories.

faq.question: How should I prepare my prompt?
faq.answer: Provide the audience, business question, metrics, timeframe, known caveats, and the decision you want to support.
